summary: Legacy static Workbench assets are no longer served by tools/workbench-server.mjs by default. Next.js gates now verify the mounted App Router runtime, the primary frontend-acceptance closeout gate, a mounted browser-events API writeback probe, the full browser-events closeout replay, scheduler dispatch writeback browser verification, and the current shell source contract. Legacy static assets still cannot be deleted because legacy frontend-acceptance compatibility, fallback-retention docs, and the explicit legacy static opt-in route have not yet been retired or quarantined.

evidence: test/workbench-server.test.js: workbench server is API-only by default for page routes
test/workbench-server.test.js: workbench server can serve legacy static shell only when explicitly enabled

tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs.project_mount_root_when_legacy_disabled: 404 JSON: workbench pages are served by Next.js

acceptance_gate_dependencies.legacy_static_requirement: none; full browser-events closeout now replays the success/failure/provider/scheduler/lifecycle/projected-loop/mobile scenarios through the mounted Next.js App Router runtime and mounted API rewrite
none; primary frontend-acceptance now runs against the mounted Next.js App Router runtime and reuses the existing artifact validator
compatibility-only legacy desktop.html/mobile.html content acceptance; no longer the primary closeout frontend-acceptance gate
fixture-level frontend acceptance assertions still cover legacy desktop.html/mobile.html routes plus mounted Next favicon compatibility
none; verifies scheduler dispatch writeback through mounted Next.js App Router desktop and mobile viewports while preserving strict raw projection pass/3-step assertions

none; asserts Next.js/Ant Design shell source contract, mounted runtime replacement gates, operation readouts, projection API/hook contracts, and responsive overflow checks without reading desktop.html/mobile.html/workbench.js/styles.css

next_served_route_replacement_gates.replaces_requirement: Next.js Workbench served route verified at /projects/ai-control-platform/ for desktop and mobile viewports
does_not_replace: tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs legacy lifecycle cleanup and timeout browser-event closeout scenarios

next_served_route_replacement_gates.replaces_requirement: Next browser-events mounted runtime and API writeback probe verified; does not replace the full browser-events closeout gate yet

Full browser-events closeout replay migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to the mounted Next.js App Router runtime while preserving lifecycle cleanup, projected loop, partial reviewer shard, terminal next-action, resume, mobile, and failure-writeback scenarios.

Primary frontend-acceptance closeout gate migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to the mounted Next.js App Router runtime with the existing frontend-acceptance validator and durable release evidence.
Scheduler dispatch writeback browser verification migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to mounted Next.js App Router desktop and mobile viewports.

Current shell gate migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html/workbench.js/styles.css DOM/CSS invariants to Next.js App Router shell source contracts plus mounted runtime replacement-gate assertions.
does_not_replace: test/frontend-acceptance.test.js fixture-level legacy frontend acceptance assertions
tools/workbench-server.mjs explicit legacy static opt-in route

retirement.reason: The Next.js served route, primary frontend-acceptance gate, mounted browser-events API writeback probe, full browser-events closeout replay, scheduler writeback browser verification, and shell source contract are now verified. Legacy assets remain compatibility and rollback dependencies until fallback-retention docs are updated, legacy frontend-acceptance fixtures are retired or quarantined, and the remaining static opt-in path is quarantined outside public runtime.
required_evidence_before_delete: FRONTEND_REFACTOR_CONSTRAINTS.md and FRONTEND_MIGRATION_INVENTORY.md updated to remove fallback-retention requirements
tools/workbench-server.mjs and tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs no longer expose opt-in legacy static serving, or the opt-in route is explicitly quarantined outside public runtime
